prompt:
You are a Document Term Memory Resolver for document-level machine translation.

You run after one or more translation batches have produced bilingual
observations. Your job is to decide whether active Document Term Memory should
be updated.

Return compact JSON only. Do not translate the document. Do not repair the
document text directly.

Important boundary:
- You do not mutate memory directly.
- You only return actions.
- Backend code validates and applies the actions.
- Prefer "no_update" when evidence is weak.
- Only propose actions for source terms that appear in the current resolver
  input's observed source text.
- Do not replace old targets by deleting them. If a target should no longer be
  used, deprecate it or mark it as avoid and optionally point to superseded_by.
- If a source term has multiple document-local meanings, add a new sense instead
  of overwriting the old one.
- Do not promote a target if it conflicts with an existing avoid/deprecated
  target.
- Do not narrow a general source term into a role label. For example, if the
  source term is "Agency", do not mark "기관 사용자" as the preferred target for
  "Agency"; that belongs to "Agency User".
- For high-risk terms such as Deposit-family, Agency, Check Capture, and
  Self-Service Kiosk, prefer no_update unless the observed source/target pair
  directly supports the exact source term.
- For Korean Deposit-family terms in Treasury/collection workflows, avoid
  promoting savings-account renderings such as "예금" unless the source context
  explicitly means a retail savings product.

Allowed actions:
- no_update
- add_sense
- update_sense
- set_active_sense
- add_target_candidate
- mark_preferred
- mark_avoid
- deprecate_target
- request_repair

Action guidance:
- add_sense: use when a new document-local meaning is discovered.
- update_sense: use when an existing sense needs more precise meaning/evidence.
- set_active_sense: use when the current document/chunk should prefer one sense.
- add_target_candidate: use when a target has been observed but is not yet
  preferred.
- mark_preferred: use when evidence supports a preferred target for first
  translation consistency.
- mark_avoid: use for target-language mistakes that should not be used.
- deprecate_target: use when a previously observed/preferred target should be
  preserved in history but no longer used.
- request_repair: use when prior translated scopes likely need repair. Do not
  repair here; only record the request.

Return JSON shape:
{
  "resolver_type": "document_term_memory_resolver",
  "actions": [
    {
      "type": "add_sense",
      "source_term": "",
      "source_terms": [],
      "sense_id": "",
      "meaning": "",
      "preferred_target": "",
      "status": "analysis_candidate",
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "evidence": "",
      "evidence_refs": [],
      "reason": "",
      "set_active": true
    }
  ],
  "caveats": []
}

TARGET_LANGUAGE:
Korean

      "translation_unit_id": 107,
      "chunk_id": "docx:page:10",
      "source_text": "Check 21, also known as the Check Clearing for the 21st Century Act, was signed into law on October 28, 2003. Provisions of the law took effect on October 28, 2004. It is important to understand the effects of Check 21 on OTCnet. Check 21 provides the legal framework for the creation of substitute checks, which can be used in place of the original paper document without an agreement in place with other financial institutions. A substitute check is a paper reproduction of the original check.",
      "translated_text": "21 세기 수표 clearing 법 (Check 21) 은 2003 년 10 월 28 일 법으로 제정되었으며, 해당 법의 조항은 2004 년 10 월 28 일 발효되었습니다. OTCnet 에 미치는 Check 21 의 영향을 이해하는 것이 중요합니다. Check 21 은 다른 금융기관과의 별도 합의 없이도 원래의 종이 문서를 대체하여 사용할 수 있는 대체 수표 (substitute check) 의 생성을 위한 법적 틀을 제공합니다. 대체 수표란 원래 수표를 종이로 복제한 것입니다.",

      "context_text": "PREVIOUS: What is Check 21?\nNEXT: To meet legal requirements, a substitute check must:",

      "context_scope": "docx:page:10",
      "context_text": "PREVIOUS: Check 21, also known as the Check Clearing for the 21st Century Act, was signed into law on October 28, 2003. Provisions of the law took effect on October 28, 2004.       "context_scope": "docx:page:10",

It also requir",

Check 21 requires financial institutions to provide education to individual consumers on substitute checks and consumer re-credit rights.",
      "translated_text": "Check 21 은 금융기관이 제시 기관으로부터 대체 수표를 수락하고, 해당 대체 수표가 규정된 요건을 충족할 경우 원래 수표와 동등한 지위를 부여하도록 요구합니다. 또한 이 법은 재변환 은행 (reconverting bank) 이 법제화 및 후속 규정을 통해 제정된 보증 및 배상 의무를 준수하도록 규정합니다. 또한 Check 21 은 금융기관이 개인 소비자에게 대체 수표 및 소비자 재신용 권리에 대한 교육을 제공하도록 요구합니다.",

document_profile.domain: Government Financial Services, Treasury Operations, Payment Processing
document_profile.workflow: Check Capture, Check Processing, Deposit Reporting, Card Processing via Self-Service Kiosks and Online portals
document_profile.purpose: To define system elements, process flows, and user role permissions for agencies and financial institutions interacting with the US Treasury.
document_profile.audience: Agency Users (Government), Financial Institution (FI) Staff, Federal Reserve Bank (FRB) Staff, System Administrators
document_profile.content_structure: Hierarchical sections covering Introduction, Process Flows (Figures), End Users, User Roles (Tables), and System Tasks (Tables).

domain_context: The document describes the 'OTCnet' system used for collecting US and foreign funds (checks, cash, cards) for the Treasury.
Key workflows involve converting paper checks to electronic debits (Check 21), processing card payments via kiosks, and reporting deposits.
The ecosystem involves three main participant groups: Agencies (collectors), Financial Institutions (FI) where checks are drawn, and Federal Reserve Banks (FRB) acting as intermediaries/settlers.

Data flows to external systems including CIR (Collections Information Repository), TCMS (Treasury Cash Management System), CARS (Central Accounting Reporting System), and MVD (Master Verification Database).

active_sense.evidence: Observed in phrases 'MICR line corrections', 'make MICR corrections', and 'Authorize MICR Correction'. The Korean translation consistently retains the English acronym 'MICR' followed by Korean nouns (e.g., 'MICR 라인', 'MICR 수정'), indicating that transliteration or translation of the acronym itself is not standard practice in this domain. Full form identified in source snippet: 'magnetic Ink character recognition'.
active_sense.meaning: Magnetic Ink Character Recognition; a technology used to verify the legitimacy of paper documents, specifically checks, by reading characters printed with magnetic ink. In this context, it refers to the data line on a check that may require correction or authorization.

senses.evidence: Observed in phrases 'MICR line corrections', 'make MICR corrections', and 'Authorize MICR Correction'. The Korean translation consistently retains the English acronym 'MICR' followed by Korean nouns (e.g., 'MICR 라인', 'MICR 수정'), indicating that transliteration or translation of the acronym itself is not standard practice in this domain. Full form identified in source snippet: 'magnetic Ink character recognition'.
senses.meaning: Magnetic Ink Character Recognition; a technology used to verify the legitimacy of paper documents, specifically checks, by reading characters printed with magnetic ink. In this context, it refers to the data line on a check that may require correction or authorization.

target_candidates.reason: Evidence confirms 'MICR' is used as an untransliterated acronym in Korean technical documentation for this system. The full form was explicitly defined in the source text ('magnetic Ink character recognition'). The sense status is upgraded from 'analysis_hint' to 'confirmed' with the preferred target set to the original acronym based on observed usage.

occurrences.translated_snippet: ACH 네트워크는 NACHA(전국 자동 결제 협회) 운영 규칙에 의해 관리되는 전국적 배치 (batch) 기반 전자 자금 이체 시스템으로, 참여 예금 취급 금융 기관 간의 전자 결제 청산을 제공합니다. 연방준비제도 (Federal Reserve) 와 전자 결제 네트워크 (Electronic Payments Network) 는 금융 기관이 ACH 입출금 내역을 송수신하는 중앙 청산 시설인 ACH 운영자 역할을 합니다. ACH 결제에는 다음이 포함됩니다:

occurrences.source_snippet: Display a magnetic Ink character recognition (MICR) line containing information appearing on the MICR line of the original check.

occurrences.translated_snippet: 이 역할의 기관 사용자는 수표를 배치로 스캔하고, 여러 회계 코드를 사용하여 항목 수준에서 수표를 조회 및 분류하거나 요약 수준에서 배치 단위로 분류하며, 배치를 마감하고, 배치를 편집하고, 수표 금액을 조정하며, 배치 마감 시 배치 제어 값을 입력할 수 있는 능력을 갖게 됩니다. 이 사용자는 OTCnet 로컬 브리지 (OLB) 인증서를 설치하고, OLB 애플리케이션을 설치하며, OLB 프로필을 생성하고, OLB 애플리케이션을 시작하고 중지할 수 있는 권한이 있습니다. 또한 이 사용자는 품질이 낮은 수표를 수락하고, MICR 라인을 수정하며, 중복 수표를 수락할 수 있는 권한이 있습니다. 이 사용자는 만료된 LVD 를 사용할 수 있는 권한이 없습니다. 이 역할은 요약 수준 분류 및 기관 코멘트와 관련된 엔드포인트의 처리 옵션을 설정, 유지 및 조회할 수 있습니다.

occurrences.source_snippet: However, the user does not have authorization to accept duplicates, make MICR corrections, authorize the use of out-of-date LVD, or accept checks with poor quality.
occurrences.surrounding_source: SECTION_HEADING: Topic 2. OTCnet User Roles TABLE_TITLE: Table 3. OTCnet Agency and FI/FRB User Roles PREVIOUS: Check Capture Operator NEXT: Agency PLSA
occurrences.translated_snippet: 그러나 이 사용자는 중복 항목을 수락하거나, MICR 수정을 수행하거나, 만료된 LVD 사용을 승인하거나, 품질이 낮은 수표를 수락할 권한이 없습니다.

observed_translations.source_text: Check 21, also known as the Check Clearing for the 21st Century Act, was signed into law on October 28, 2003. Provisions of the law took effect on October 28, 2004. It is important to understand the effects of Check 21 on OTCnet. Check 21 provides the legal framework for the creation of substitute checks, which can be used in place of the original paper document without an agreement in place with other financial institutions. A substitute check is a paper reproduction of the original check.
observed_translations.translated_text: 21 세기 수표 clearing 법 (Check 21) 은 2003 년 10 월 28 일 법으로 제정되었으며, 해당 법의 조항은 2004 년 10 월 28 일 발효되었습니다. OTCnet 에 미치는 Check 21 의 영향을 이해하는 것이 중요합니다. Check 21 은 다른 금융기관과의 별도 합의 없이도 원래의 종이 문서를 대체하여 사용할 수 있는 대체 수표 (substitute check) 의 생성을 위한 법적 틀을 제공합니다. 대체 수표란 원래 수표를 종이로 복제한 것입니다.
observed_translations.context_text:
PREVIOUS: What is Check 21?
NEXT: To meet legal requirements, a substitute check must:

NEXT: Overall, this legislation has modernized the nation’s check payments system. Check 21 is designed to foster innovation in the payments system and to enhance its efficiency by reducing some of the legal impediments to che

observed_translations.source_text: Overall, this legislation has modernized the nation’s check payments system. Check 21 is designed to foster innovation in the payments system and to enhance its efficiency by reducing some of the legal impediments to check truncation.
observed_translations.translated_text: 전반적으로 이 법안은 미국의 수표 결제 시스템을 현대화했습니다. Check 21 은 수표 절단 (check truncation) 에 대한 일부 법적 장애물을 줄여 결제 시스템의 혁신을 촉진하고 효율성을 높이기 위해 고안되었습니다.
observed_translations.context_text:
PREVIOUS: Be suitable for automated processing in the same manner as the original check.
NEXT: ALL payment instruments are eligible for processing under OTCnet, including business checks, money orders, Treasury checks, credit card checks, traveler’s checks, cashier’s checks, official checks, third-party checks, pa

observed_translations.source_text: ALL payment instruments are eligible for processing under OTCnet, including business checks, money orders, Treasury checks, credit card checks, traveler’s checks, cashier’s checks, official checks, third-party checks, payroll checks and checks drawn on state or local government accounts.
observed_translations.translated_text: OTCnet 을 통해 처리할 수 있는 모든 지급 수단에는 사업자 수표, 우편환, 재무부 수표, 신용카드 수표, 여행자 수표, 현금수표, 관공서 수표, 제 3 자 수표, 급여 수표 및 주 또는 지방 정부 계좌에서 발행된 수표가 포함됩니다.
observed_translations.context_text:
PREVIOUS: Overall, this legislation has modernized the nation’s check payments system. Check 21 is designed to foster innovation in the payments system and to enhance its efficiency by reducing some of the legal impediments to che
NEXT: Check 21 requires financial institutions to accept a substitute check from a presenting institute and grant it equivalent status to the original check if the substitute check meets prescribed requirements. It also requir

observed_translations.source_text: Check 21 requires financial institutions to accept a substitute check from a presenting institute and grant it equivalent status to the original check if the substitute check meets prescribed requirements. It also requires a reconverting bank to meet the warranties and indemnities enacted through legislation and subsequent regulations. Check 21 requires financial institutions to provide education to individual consumers on substitute checks and consumer re-credit rights.
observed_translations.translated_text: Check 21 은 금융기관이 제시 기관으로부터 대체 수표를 수락하고, 해당 대체 수표가 규정된 요건을 충족할 경우 원래 수표와 동등한 지위를 부여하도록 요구합니다. 또한 이 법은 재변환 은행 (reconverting bank) 이 법제화 및 후속 규정을 통해 제정된 보증 및 배상 의무를 준수하도록 규정합니다. 또한 Check 21 은 금융기관이 개인 소비자에게 대체 수표 및 소비자 재신용 권리에 대한 교육을 제공하도록 요구합니다.
observed_translations.context_text:
PREVIOUS: ALL payment instruments are eligible for processing under OTCnet, including business checks, money orders, Treasury checks, credit card checks, traveler’s checks, cashier’s checks, official checks, third-party checks, pa
NEXT: For more information on Check 21, visit: https://www.frbservices.org/education/products-services-education/check21-act.html
